intro: Tetraena migiurtinorum, also known as the Socotra aloe or dragon tree, is a succulent plant species native to the Socotra Islands in Yemen. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including wound healing, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which may aid in treating skin conditions and reducing inflammation. Bioactive constituents of Tetraena migiurtinorum include anthraquinones, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, and saponins, which possess wound-healing and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tetraena migiurtinorum include topical creams and gels, as well as oral capsules and teas, which are used to harness its medicinal properties for skin and digestive health.
